title: Eva Onegeek is down
wikitext:
[]: Eva Onegeek is down?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Thu Apr 18, 2019 2:51 pm It seems that one of the oldest resources for Eva stuff is now in the Internet graveyard. This one interview I sometimes plaster around everywhere now returns a 404 error: https://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/evang ... 03855.html It is still accessible through Web Archive, however, with very recent snapshots from less than a month ago. http://web.archive.org/web/201903282033 ... 03855.html I am asking this because I wonder if maybe there are a bunch of links to it in the Wiki or in the source archives or something that might need to be fixed and link to archived versions of the pages instead.

[]: Re: Eva Onegeek is down?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Apr 18, 2019 3:12 pm It looks like the site is trying to be back up online. There’s now a message there that says that the mailing list is back up, but that they are still missing some archives.

[]: Re: Eva Onegeek is down?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Apr 18, 2019 3:53 pm Trouble is that the links from the front page all 404, and the current front page is the same as it was 15 years ago -- https://web.archive.org/web/20040329131 ... egeek.org/ -- so there's no information about what''s going on now.

[]: Re: Eva Onegeek is down?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Apr 18, 2019 10:38 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Trouble is that the links from the front page all 404, and the current front page is the same as it was 15 years ago -- https://web.archive.org/web/20040329131 ... egeek.org/ -- so there's no information about what''s going on now.

[]: Re: Eva Onegeek is down?             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Thu Apr 25, 2019 10:56 pm I don't suppose anyone has any sort of backup or can/is interested in somehow taking over for him? Or are we going to depend on Web Archive? I don't know what might be missing from there, and there is the aforementioned problem of many Onegeek sources being linked and used everywhere.

Article: Eva Onegeek is down
25, 2019 10:56 pm I don't suppose anyone has any sort of backup or can/is interested in somehow taking over for him? Or are we going to depend on Web Archive? I don't know what might be missing from there, and there is the aforementioned problem of many Onegeek sources being linked and used everywhere.
